title: Repository advertises sample Prometheus/Loki alert rules and Grafana dashboards but contains none
suggested_labels: Type: Documentation; good first issue; Status: Help Wanted
summary: The GitHub repo description and README both state the repo 'contains samples of Prometheus and Loki alert rules, and Grafana dashboards', but the entire repository (only branch: main, single 'Initial commit') tracks just two files: LICENSE and README.md. There are no .yaml/.yml alert rules and no .json Grafana dashboards anywhere in the tree or git history. The repository is effectively an empty stub that does not deliver the content it advertises.
why_good_first_issue: Very approachable: the task is to add the promised sample files (a Prometheus rules YAML, a Loki rules YAML, a Grafana dashboard JSON) and/or update the README to reflect the actual contents and intended structure. No build system, no code, no toolchain needed; great first contribution for someone learning the COS rule/dashboard formats. Newly added YAML/JSON can be validated with simple parsers.
affected_files: README.md (only describes intent, 2 lines); LICENSE; (no rules/ or dashboards/ directories exist)
repro_steps: 1) Clone repo (depth 80). 2) git ls-remote --heads origin -> only 'main'. 3) git log --oneline --all -> single commit 'e66d774 Initial commit'. 4) git ls-files -> exactly LICENSE and README.md. 5) git log --all --name-only --diff-filter=A | sort -u -> only LICENSE and README.md ever added (no rules/dashboards in history). 6) find . -type f -not -path './.git/*' -> ./LICENSE ./README.md. 7) gh api repos/canonical/cos-configuration-rules-dashboards --jq .description -> 'This repo contains samples of Prometheus and Loki alert rules, and Grafana dashboards'. Mismatch between advertised content and actual content confirmed.
repro_result: Confirmed by inspection on VM: repository has a single branch (main) and a single 'Initial commit'; the only tracked files are LICENSE and README.md (git ls-files), and no other file has ever existed in history (git log --diff-filter=A). No Prometheus/Loki rule YAML or Grafana dashboard JSON is present despite the README and repo description promising them.
fix_hint: Either populate the repo with the advertised samples (e.g. rules/prometheus/*.yaml, rules/loki/*.yaml, dashboards/*.json) with a short index in the README, or, if samples will come later, update the README to describe the intended layout and current status. Adding even one validated example of each artifact type would satisfy the repo's stated purpose. Any added YAML/JSON can be validated with python3 -c 'import yaml,json; ...'.
notes: Stack: rules (YAML) + dashboards (JSON) repo, but currently no content. 0 open issues, so self-identified. No Go/TF needed. Inspection tier (nothing to run). This is the central issue with the repo.

title: README is a one-line stub with no usage, structure, or COS integration guidance
summary: README.md is only a title plus a single sentence (123 bytes, 2 lines). It does not explain what the samples are for, how they map to the Canonical Observability Stack, how to consume them (e.g. with cos-configuration-k8s charm), the expected directory layout, or contribution guidance. For a samples repo whose value is documentation, this is a meaningful gap.
why_good_first_issue: Single-file Markdown change with clear scope and no risk; ideal for a first-time contributor. Improves discoverability and usability of the repo immediately.
affected_files: README.md (entire file: '# cos-configuration-rules-dashboards' + 'This repo contains samples of Prometheus and Loki alert rules, and Grafana dashboards')
repro_steps: 1) cat README.md -> only a heading and one descriptive sentence. 2) wc -l -c README.md -> 2 lines, 123 bytes. 3) No sections for Usage, Structure, How the samples integrate with COS (e.g. via the cos-configuration-k8s charm), or Contributing. 4) No links to the COS docs or to the consuming charm.
repro_result: Confirmed by inspection: README.md is 2 lines / 123 bytes containing only a title and a single sentence (verified via cat and wc). No usage/structure/integration/contribution content exists.
fix_hint: Expand README.md with: a short purpose paragraph; an explanation of how the rules/dashboards are intended to be consumed (e.g. by the cos-configuration-k8s charm / cos-configuration repo); the planned directory structure; and links to relevant Canonical Observability docs. Keep it concise but useful.
notes: Secondary self-identified issue, complements the 'repo is empty' finding. Inspection tier. If only one issue is wanted, prefer the first (missing content) row.
